NCT00262600.name: RE-LY
NCT00262600.group: Dabigatran 150 mg BID | DOAC (non-valvular AF; vs Warfarin INR 2-3; RE-LY)

allOutcomes.title: Stroke or systemic embolism dabigatran 150 mg vs warfarin (RE-LY primary; median 2.0 yr)

NCT00262600.snippet: Connolly SJ et al. NEJM 2009;361:1139-1151 (RE-LY). First DOAC superiority signal; 150 mg BID arm reported (110 mg non-inferior but excluded for primary-dose analysis).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria Patients with non-valvular atrial fibrillation (AF), at moderate to high risk of stroke, or systemic embolism with at least one additional risk factor (i.e. previous ischemic stroke, TIA, or systemic embolism, left ventricular dysfunction, age >=75 years, age >=65 with either diabetes mellitus, history of coronary artery disease or hypertension)~Exclusion criteria~1. evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: Male 3865, Male 3840, Male 3809. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

NCT00403767.name: ROCKET-AF
NCT00403767.group: Rivaroxaban 20 mg daily | DOAC (non-valvular AF with at least moderate stroke risk; vs Warfarin INR 2-3; ROCKET-AF)

allOutcomes.title: Stroke or systemic embolism rivaroxaban vs warfarin (ROCKET-AF ITT primary; median 1.9 yr)

NCT00403767.snippet: Patel MR et al. NEJM 2011;365:883-891 (ROCKET-AF). Rivaroxaban in higher-CHA2DS2-VASc population (mean ~3.5).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Patients must have documented atrial fibrillation on 2 separate occasions within 6 months before screening~* History of a prior stroke, transient ischemic attack or non-neurologic systemic embolism believed to be cardiac in origin, or at least two of the following risk factors: heart failure, hypertension, age 75 years or greater, diabetes mellitus~Exclusion Criteria:~* Significant mitral stenosis~* Transient atrial fibrillation caused by a reversible disorder~* Active int...

NCT00412984.name: ARISTOTLE
NCT00412984.group: Apixaban 5 mg BID | DOAC (non-valvular AF with at least one CV risk factor; vs Warfarin INR 2-3; ARISTOTLE)

allOutcomes.title: Stroke or systemic embolism apixaban vs warfarin (ARISTOTLE primary; median 1.8 yr)

NCT00412984.snippet: Granger CB et al. NEJM 2011;365:981-992 (ARISTOTLE). Apixaban superior to warfarin on all 3 co-primary outcomes (stroke/SE, major bleeding, all-cause mortality).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Males and females ≥ 18 yrs with atrial fibrillation (AF) and one or more of the following risk factors for stroke:~* Age ≥ 75, previous stroke~* transient ischemic attack (TIA) or Systemic Embolism (SE)~* Symptomatic congestive heart failure or left ventricular dysfunction with left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) ≤ 40%~* Diabetes mellitus or hypertension requiring pharmacological treatment"

NCT00781391.name: ENGAGE-AF-TIMI-48
NCT00781391.group: Edoxaban 60 mg daily | DOAC (non-valvular AF moderate-to-high stroke risk; vs Warfarin INR 2-3; ENGAGE-AF)

allOutcomes.title: Stroke or systemic embolism edoxaban 60 mg vs warfarin (ENGAGE-AF TIMI 48 modified-ITT primary; median 2.8 yr)

NCT00781391.snippet: Giugliano RP et al. NEJM 2013;369:2093-2104 (ENGAGE-AF TIMI 48). Edoxaban 60 mg non-inferior and numerically superior. 30 mg arm excluded from primary NMA (suboptimal dosing).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 21 Years; healthy volunteers: f. NMA_CONFIG.outcome_label: Stroke or systemic embolism (trial-primary; ITT or modified ITT)
NMA_CONFIG.note: k=4 DOAC-vs-Warfarin trials. Warfarin-star network (no head-to-head between DOACs — transitivity supports indirect DOAC-vs-DOAC comparisons via shared Warfarin control). Matches Ruff 2014 Lancet landmark NMA canonical set.

title: RapidMeta Cardiology | DOAC Class NMA vs Warfarin for Stroke Prevention in AF v1.0
